what_it_does: Runs a Node.js lint check that loads dna/canon.json, collects canon nodes and references, verifies referenced files exist, verifies functioning files are referenced by canon code refs, verifies checked functioning files carry a canon header, verifies dna face files are listed under face refs, reports pass/fail counts, and exits nonzero on failure.
observations: Uses synchronous filesystem reads and directory scans throughout.
Derives root from the module URL by stepping from engine/lint to engine to the project root.
Builds FUNCTIONING from root files, a fixed list of subdirectories, and several explicit registry/content paths.
Collects refs from canon nodes recursively across faces, docs, and code.
Splits references at # before checking file existence or canonical coverage.
Exempts a small NO_HEADER set from canon header validation.
Reports checks with console output and terminates with process.exit based on failure count.
standouts: Treats canon coverage as a mechanical contract between dna/canon.json and files on disk.
Checks both stale references and unreferenced functioning files.
Uses file headers as a second binding from implementation files back to canon nodes.
conventions: ES module script with a node shebang.
Top-level procedural lint script rather than exported library functions.
Synchronous fs APIs fit a short CLI validation command.
Status helpers mutate shared pass/fail counters and print formatted messages.
Header matching uses a compact regular expression for dotted lower-hyphen canon ids.
concerns: The fixed directory list means newly added code directories are not discovered unless this file is edited.
readdirSync on each listed directory will throw if a directory is missing, before the script can report a structured lint failure.
The comment says the functioning set is never listed by hand, while the implementation includes a hand-maintained directory list and explicit file additions.
notes: This is the canon drift lint: it checks that canon references, functioning files, headers, and face files agree with the filesystem.
questions: Should referenced docs and faces also require back-reference headers, or only functioning code files?
Should missing scanned directories be reported as lint failures instead of uncaught filesystem errors?
Is the fixed FUNCTIONING directory list intended to be the complete living code estate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implemented behavior: the script makes canon/documentation drift mechanically detectable through pointer, coverage, header, and face checks.
intention_signals: It seems intended to keep the canon registry synchronized with the living files by making missing references, stale pointers, and absent canon headers visible in an automated check.
novelty: Combines registry pointer validation with reverse coverage and per-file canon header checks in one local lint script.
contribution: Provides a concrete validation layer that binds a canon ledger to the project filesystem, preserving checks for resolved references, owned functioning files, header markings, and face registration.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js canon lint script that compares dna/canon.json against project files, checking reference resolution, code coverage, canon headers, and face registration before exiting with pass/fail status.
intention_for_embedding: It was for detecting documentation and canon drift by enforcing bidirectional links between the canon registry and functioning files.

symbols.does: Increments the pass counter and prints a checkmarked success message.
symbols.description: This helper records a passed check by mutating the shared pass count and writing a formatted line to stdout. It centralizes the success message shape used by the lint report.

symbols.does: Increments the fail counter and prints a cross-marked failure message.
symbols.description: This helper records a failed check by mutating the shared fail count and writing a formatted line to stdout. The final process exit code depends on whether this counter remains zero.
